proofs.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix a stage n and a feasible vector p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}. By def:feasible-designs, epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon for every k in I_n. Write X_i^1 = T_i(Z)/pi_i^1(p) and X_i^0 = C_i(Z)/pi_i^0(p), so D_1(p,Z) = sum_{i in O_n} X_i^1 and D_0(p,Z) = sum_{i in O_n} X_i^0 by def:hajek-denominators.

First consider the means. Under ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, the coordinates {Z_k : k in I_n} are mutually independent with E_p Z_k = p_k. Hence, for each i,
E_p T_i(Z) = E_p product_{k in N_i(G_n)} Z_k = product_{k in N_i(G_n)} p_k = pi_i^1(p),
with the usual empty-product convention. Therefore E_p X_i^1 = 1. Similarly,
E_p C_i(Z) = E_p product_{k in N_i(G_n)} (1-Z_k) = product_{k in N_i(G_n)} (1-p_k) = pi_i^0(p),
so E_p X_i^0 = 1. Summing over the n outcome units gives E_p[D_1(p,Z)] = E_p[D_0(p,Z)] = n.

We next bound the variances uniformly over feasible p. If S_{ij}(G_n) is empty, then X_i^a and X_j^a, for a in {0,1}, are functions of disjoint sets of independent assignment coordinates, so Cov_p(X_i^a,X_j^a)=0. If S_{ij}(G_n) is nonempty, ass:bounded-outcome-degree gives |N_i(G_n)| <= dbar and |N_j(G_n)| <= dbar. Since p is feasible, pi_i^1(p), pi_j^1(p), pi_i^0(p), and pi_j^0(p) are all at least epsilon^{dbar}. Consequently 0 <= X_i^a <= epsilon^{-dbar} for a in {0,1}, and therefore
|Cov_p(X_i^a,X_j^a)| <= E_p|X_i^a X_j^a| + E_p X_i^a E_p X_j^a <= epsilon^{-2 dbar} + 1.
For each fixed i, ass:bounded-overlap-dependency gives at most Delta_n <= Dbar indices j with S_{ij}(G_n) nonempty. Thus the number of ordered pairs (i,j) that can have nonzero covariance is at most n Dbar. It follows that, for a in {0,1},
Var_p(D_a(p,Z)) = sum_{i,j in O_n} Cov_p(X_i^a,X_j^a) <= n Dbar (epsilon^{-2 dbar}+1)
in absolute value, and hence Var_p(D_1(p,Z)) = O(n) and Var_p(D_0(p,Z)) = O(n), uniformly over p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}. The implicit constant depends only on epsilon, dbar, and Dbar.

Finally, Chebyshev's inequality converts the variance bound into the stated concentration. For every M>0 and a in {0,1},
sup_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} P_p(|D_a(p,Z)/n - 1| > M n^{-1/2}) <= Dbar (epsilon^{-2 dbar}+1) / M^2.
This is exactly D_a(p,Z)/n = 1 + O_p(n^{-1/2}) uniformly over feasible p, for a=1 and a=0. Combining the mean, variance, and concentration statements proves the lemma.
